name: rtl-impl
description: Legacy MAS RTL checklist. SSOT flows must use the dynamic rtl_todo_plan, not this fixed checklist.

tasks.content: [FROM MAS] Read MAS handoff + extract all RTL requirements
tasks.detail:
This task is initiated from a [MAS HANDOFF] from mas-gen.

STEP 1 — Find the MAS file. Look for: <ip_name>/mas/<ip_name>_mas.md. If ip_name is unknown, scan the current directory for */mas/*_mas.md. Confirm the MAS has all §1–§9 sections.

STEP 2 — Extract from each section:
- §2: Module Hierarchy + port list (name/width/direction/clock domain), parameters
- §3: Feature Operation + FSM state table (states/transitions/outputs)
- §4: Register FAM (offset/name/width/access/reset)
- §5: Interrupt sources + enable/status/clear logic
- §6: Memory instances (type/depth/width/latency)
- §7: Timing + CDC crossings
- §8: RTL coding style rules

STEP 3 — Create RTL file skeleton: <ip_name>/rtl/<ip_name>.sv (empty module stub). Do not implement anything yet.

Gate: MAS fully read, RTL file created.

tasks.content: Write module header: parameters and port declarations
tasks.detail: module <name> #(params) (ports); Match §2 exactly. All ports with explicit direction+type+width. Use SystemVerilog ANSI port types by default: input logic, output logic, and inout wire for bidirectional nets. Parameterize user-tunable input/output shape from MAS/SSOT, such as data width, address width, input/output channel count, lane count, and depth. Use clear parameter names and derive port/internal widths from them instead of scattering numeric literals.

Implement all registers from MAS §4 FAM table. RW registers: written on APB/AXI write. RO registers: driven by internal logic. RW1C registers (interrupt status): cleared when CPU writes 1. Nonblocking (<=) in always @(posedge clk ...) with reset from §8.

Implement FSM exactly as defined in MAS §3 state table. Use the conventional explicit FSM style by default: localparam state encodings, logic state/next_state signals, one always @(posedge clk ...) state-register block, and one always @(*) next-state/output-decode block with defaults. If MAS/SSOT or the user specifies another synthesizable FSM style, follow that explicit style. All states must be reachable. Add default clause.

Implement each feature's datapath as described in MAS §3. Blocking (=) in always @(*). Default assignment at top of each block to prevent latches. Case statements with default. Derive datapath widths, masks, counters, and slices from named parameters/localparams tied to the in/out interface. Prefer exact shift-based arithmetic for multiply/divide: powers of two use << / >>, exact constant scaling may use shift-add/subtract, and general * or / requires explicit MAS/SSOT justification. Add concise comments for non-obvious datapath, parameter, and arithmetic choices so reviewers can understand the hardware intent.
Implement all interrupt sources from MAS §5 table. irq output = OR of (INT_STATUS[i] & INT_ENABLE[i]) for all sources. W1C clear: INT_STATUS[i] <= INT_STATUS[i] & ~apb_wdata[i] on write.
Instantiate all memories from MAS §6 table. Use appropriate type (SRAM macro / FIFO). Verify latency matches §6 and timing matches §7.
Run /lint <module>.sv. Fix all errors. Warnings: fix width mismatches; document others with // lint_waive comments.
